name: ValidateCommand
purpose: Validate that the command is one of the four allowed values and that required fields in message_data are present based on command type.
behavior: Check command against allowed set; for each command, verify required keys in message_data (e.g., channel_id for send/history/join, channel_name for create_channel, content for send). Return error if invalid.

semantic_outputs.name: validation_result
semantic_outputs.description: Boolean indicating valid or invalid, plus error message if invalid

guarantees: Only valid commands and required fields proceed
stop_reason: Pure validation logic, no further decomposition needed

outputs.name: validation_result
outputs.description: Dictionary with success (bool), data (None), message (str) indicating valid or error

signature: def ValidateCommand(command: str, message_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Command must be one of: send, history, create_channel, join
message_data must contain required fields based on command type

acceptance_criteria.description: Valid command with required fields returns success
Invalid command returns error with message
Missing required fields returns error with message

name: SendMessage
purpose: Handle the 'send' command: verify user membership, store message, update user's last_seen, notify other channel members.
behavior: Check if user_id is in channel's members list; if not, return error. Generate new message_id, store message in messages store with timestamp. Update user's last_seen in users store. Notify other channel members (simulated as adding to a notification list or logging). Return success with message data.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel data store for membership check

semantic_outputs.name: send_result
semantic_outputs.description: Dictionary with success, data (message details), message

postconditions: Message stored if user is member
stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check, store, update, notify

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel data store for membership check

outputs.name: send_result
outputs.description: Dictionary with success (bool), data (dict with message_id, content, timestamp, channel_id, user_id), message (str)

signature: def SendMessage(message_data: dict, channels: dict, messages: dict, users: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: User must be a member of the channel
Message must have content and channel_id

acceptance_criteria.description: Message stored successfully when user is member
Error returned when user is not a member

name: GetHistory
purpose: Handle the 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from a channel sorted by timestamp descending.
behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return as list.

semantic_outputs.name: history_result
semantic_outputs.description: Dictionary with success, data (list of messages), message

stop_reason: Atomic read operation with sorting and limit

outputs.name: history_result
outputs.description: Dictionary with success (bool), data (list of message dicts), message (str)

signature: def GetHistory(message_data: dict, messages: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: channel_id must be provided in message_data

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns up to 100 messages sorted by timestamp descending
Returns empty list if no messages in channel

name: CreateChannel
purpose: Handle the 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set caller as creator and first member.
behavior: Check if channel_name already exists in channels store; if so, return error. Generate new channel_id, store channel with name, members list containing user_id, created_by set to user_id. Return success with channel data.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel data store for reading and writing

semantic_outputs.name: create_result
semantic_outputs.description: Dictionary with success, data (channel details), message

postconditions: New channel created if name unique
User is first member and creator
stop_reason: Single atomic operation with uniqueness check

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel data store for reading and writing

outputs.name: create_result
outputs.description: Dictionary with success (bool), data (dict with channel_id, channel_name, members, created_by), message (str)

signature: def CreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Check uniqueness and store new channel

data_operations.description: Read existing channels for uniqueness check, write new channel

constraints.description: channel_name must be provided in message_data

acceptance_criteria.description: New channel created with unique name
Error returned if channel name already exists
Creator is added as first member

name: JoinChannel
purpose: Handle the 'join' command: add user to channel's member list if not already a member.
behavior: Check if user_id is already in channel's members list; if so, return error. Otherwise, add user_id to members list in channels store. Return success with updated member list.

semantic_inputs.name: channels
semantic_inputs.description: Channel data store for reading and writing

semantic_outputs.name: join_result
semantic_outputs.description: Dictionary with success, data (updated member list), message

postconditions: User added to channel members if not already member
stop_reason: Single atomic operation with membership check

inputs.name: channels
inputs.description: Channel data store for reading and writing

outputs.name: join_result
outputs.description: Dictionary with success (bool), data (dict with channel_id, members list), message (str)

signature: def JoinChannel(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read members list and update it

data_operations.description: Read channel members, write updated members list

constraints.description: User must not already be a member of the channel
channel_id must be provided in message_data

acceptance_criteria.description: User added to channel members if not already a member
Error returned if user is already a member
